mechanism_narrative: Increases in the federal funds rate directly raise the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS increases over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales exceeding 460 billion dollars indicates sustained consumer spending strength that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >55% of instances where RSAFS crosses the threshold, or rolling Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in the unemployment rate and decline in total nonfarm payrolls signals labor market deterioration that reduces aggregate income and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with >50% accuracy over a 2-year out-of-sample period, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects supply chain normalization and capacity utilization recovery, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months with rising INDPRO, or the signal loses predictive power during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 240 force central banks to accelerate rate hikes, increasing discount rates and compressing equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic expansion that lifts corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no statistical significance in predicting 60-day returns, or forward returns are negative in >50% of synchronized growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls expand household income and consumer spending capacity, supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative correlation with payroll growth over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 102 indicates manufacturing contraction and supply bottlenecks that disrupt corporate output, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of instances where INDPRO drops below 102, or the threshold signal fails to achieve a positive Sharpe ratio in walk-forward testing.
